passages.passage_text: Each of the question below consists of a question and three statements numbered I, II and III given below it. You have to decide whether the data provided in the statement are sufficient to answer the question.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Is the average age of the men less than 32?

choices.a: One-third of the men are younger than 25.
choices.b: One-half of the men are between 25 and 30.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If John is exactly 4 yr older than Sunita, how old is John?

choices.a: Exactly 9 yr ago John was 5 times as old as Sunita was then.
choices.b: Sunita is more than 9 yr old.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: How many minutes does the clock lose a day?

choices.a: The clock reads 6:00 when it is really 5:48.
choices.b: The clock is 40 s fast each hour.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Ram and Esha are in a line to purchase tickets. How many people are in the line?

choices.a: There are 20 people behind Ram and 20 people in front of Esha.
choices.b: There are 5 people between Ram and Esha.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: How much time will a computer need to solve 150 problems?

choices.a: The computer needs 50 s to solve the first problem.
choices.b: A man needs 6 h to solve the problems.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If x and y are positive integers and x is a multiple of y is y = 2?

choices.b: x + 2 is a multiple of y.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: How many daughters does of W have?

choices.a: I and III are sufficient to answer the question
choices.b: All I, II and III are required to answer the question
choices.c: II and III are sufficient to answer the question
choices.d: Question cannot be answered even with all I, II and III
choices.e: I and II are sufficient to answer the question

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Who among A, B, C, D, E and F, each having a different height is the tallest?

choices.a: I and II are sufficient to answer the question
choices.b: I and III are sufficient to answer the question
choices.c: II and III are sufficient to answer the question
choices.d: All I, II and III are required to answer the question
choices.e: All I, II and III even together are not sufficient to answer the question

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Towards which direction is Village J from Village W?

choices.a: Only III is sufficient to answer the question
choices.b: II and III sufficient to answer the question
choices.c: All I, II and III are required to answer the question
choices.d: Question cannot be answered even with all I, II and III

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: How is 'go' written in a code language?

choices.a: I and III are sufficient to answer the question
choices.b: II and III are sufficient to answer the question
choices.c: I and II are sufficient to answer the question
choices.d: All I, II and III are required to answer the question
